home.heroDesc: Build secure, scalable identity infrastructure for modern applications. SSO, MFA, multi-tenancy, and audit compliance — so identity never becomes a bottleneck
home.featuresSubtitle: From authentication to access control, from audit compliance to developer integration — AuthMS covers the full identity lifecycle
home.featureSsoDesc: SAML 2.0, OIDC, OAuth 2.0 protocols — one-click enterprise directory integration
home.featureMfaDesc: TOTP, SMS, Email, Passkey / WebAuthn, backup recovery codes — all-round protection

home.featureHaDesc: 21 microservices independently deployed, high-availability architecture design

home.securityGlobalDesc: Security by design, from day one

cookie.necessaryDesc: Required for the website to function properly and cannot be disabled
cookie.analyticsDesc: Help us understand how visitors interact with the website

features.pageDesc: AuthMS provides enterprise-grade capabilities covering the full identity lifecycle, from authentication to authorization, audit to compliance
features.title: Full-Featured Platform
features.subtitle: AuthMS provides enterprise-grade capabilities covering the full identity lifecycle — from authentication to authorization, audit to compliance, all in one place
features.ssoDesc: Supports SAML 2.0, OpenID Connect (OIDC), and OAuth 2.0 industry standards. Users sign in once to access all authorized applications, while admins manage all app integrations from a unified dashboard
features.mfaDesc: From TOTP to Passkey, from SMS to Email — AuthMS provides a complete MFA stack. Adaptive MFA dynamically adjusts authentication strength based on risk scoring, balancing security and user experience
features.passkeyDesc: Pioneering FIDO2/WebAuthn Passkey support. Users authenticate with biometrics (fingerprint, face) or hardware security keys, eliminating password leak risks entirely
features.tenantDesc: Purpose-built for SaaS and B2B scenarios. Each tenant has completely isolated data spaces, independent branding, custom domains, and flexible org structures with member management
features.auditDesc: Complete audit logging with hash-chain verification ensuring immutability. Built-in GDPR DSAR processing, SoD separation of duties, and ISO27001 control mapping
features.sessionDesc: Real-time visibility into active sessions and trusted devices. Support for remote logout and device revocation. Device fingerprint-based risk scoring detects anomalous login behavior
features.developerDesc: 544+ RESTful API endpoints covering every business scenario. Interactive API docs, SDK quick starts, and a Token debugger. gRPC interfaces for high-performance internal service communication
features.billingDesc: Multi-tier plans, usage-based billing, invoice management, revenue deferral, and tax exports. Tenants self-upgrade/downgrade; platform operators access global revenue reports.
features.notificationDesc: Unified notification template management supporting email, SMS, in-app notifications, and Push. Users self-configure preferences; platform sends broadcast and targeted messages
features.storageDesc: Chunked uploads, version management, watermarking, recycle bin, and other enterprise file management features. Backed by MinIO + SeaweedFS with private deployment and object storage extension support
features.walletDesc: Complete wallet capabilities for platforms requiring transactions: top-up, withdrawal, transfer, freezing, dispute handling. Points system with earn, spend, freeze, expiry, and rule engine
features.haDesc: 14 independent microservices deployed via Docker containers with Kubernetes auto-scaling. API gateway handles routing, auth, and rate limiting. Prometheus + Grafana for full-stack monitoring

blog.pageDesc: AuthMS Blog — Security articles, product updates, and best practices
blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Security articles, product updates, and best practices
blog.removedDesc: This article does not exist or has been removed

pricing.title: Simple, Transparent Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Whether you're an individual developer or a large enterprise, find the right plan. Scale as you grow — no hidden fees.
pricing.freeDesc: For individual developers and small projects to get started.
pricing.proDesc: For growing businesses that need full core capabilities.
pricing.enterpriseDesc: For large enterprises needing dedicated deployment and advanced compliance.
pricing.faqQ1: What is MAU? How is it calculated?
pricing.faqA1: MAU (Monthly Active Users) refers to unique users who complete at least one login or authentication operation per month. We count deduplicated users monthly.
pricing.faqQ2: Can I try Pro for free?
pricing.faqA2: Yes. New users can try Pro free for 14 days without a credit card. After the trial, you can downgrade to Free or subscribe to Pro.
pricing.faqA3: Enterprise plans support on-premises and dedicated cloud deployment. Our engineering team will assist with environment setup and migration.
pricing.faqQ4: How do I upgrade or downgrade?
pricing.faqA4: Tenant admins can upgrade or downgrade plans at any time in the Admin Console under Billing & Subscriptions. Upgrades take effect immediately; downgrades take effect in the next billing cycle.
pricing.faqA5: CNY, USD, EUR and more are supported. Pro and above plans support VAT invoicing and corporate bank transfers.
pricing.faqA6: AuthMS is pursuing ISO27001 and SOC 2 Type II certification. All data in transit uses TLS 1.3 encryption, and data at rest uses AES-256 encryption. Passwords are hashed with bcrypt/scrypt, and audit logs use hash-chain technology for tamper resistance.
pricing.pageDesc: AuthMS Pricing — Free, Pro, and Enterprise plans. Scale as you grow, no hidden fees.

customers.title: Customer Stories
customers.subtitle: See how innovative teams are building secure and reliable identity infrastructure with AuthMS.
customers.ctaTitle: Ready to be our next customer story?
customers.ctaDesc: Whether you're a startup or a large enterprise, AuthMS provides a solid foundation for your identity architecture.
customers.case1Desc: Centralized SSO and permission management across internal tools.
customers.case2Desc: Built-in audit logs with hash-chain verification for security frameworks.
customers.case3Desc: Passkey + MFA for enhanced account security.
customers.case4Desc: Multi-factor authentication and single sign-on for large-scale users.
customers.case5Desc: Private deployment options with audit logs for compliance reviews.
customers.case6Desc: Multi-tenant architecture supporting independent branding and data isolation.

about.pageDesc: Learn about the AuthMS team, mission, and values, and our innovation journey in identity security
about.title: About AuthMS
about.heroDesc: AuthMS was born from a simple belief: identity security should not be a bottleneck for innovation. We are a team of security engineers and product designers building the next generation of identity infrastructure
about.missionDesc: Make enterprise-grade identity security accessible to every application. We believe identity is the cornerstone of the digital world, and security should not come at the cost of user experience
about.value1Desc: Security is not a feature — it's the foundation of AuthMS. From design to deployment, security runs through the entire product lifecycle
about.value2Desc: API-First design philosophy. Every line of code is optimized to reduce developer integration time
about.value3Desc: Open-core architecture, hash-chain audit logs. Customers always maintain control of their data
about.value4Desc: Stay sharp on cutting-edge technology without blindly following trends or becoming complacent
about.milestone1Desc: Core team formed, technical architecture and product roadmap finalized
about.milestone2Desc: Core authentication engine completed, first internal projects onboarded
about.milestone4Desc: 21 microservices, 544+ API endpoints, initiating SOC 2 Type II certification preparation

trust.pageDesc: AuthMS Security & Compliance — Pursuing ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certification
trust.title: Security & Compliance
trust.subtitle: Security is not a feature — it's the foundation of AuthMS. From design to deployment, from code to infrastructure, security permeates every line of code and every decision
trust.highlight1Desc: Data in transit uses TLS 1.3, data at rest uses AES-256 encryption, with additional encryption layers for sensitive fields
trust.highlight2Desc: Every audit log entry is linked to the previous one via a hash chain, ensuring immutability
trust.highlight3Desc: Security in the CI/CD pipeline: automated SAST, dependency scanning, security gates on every commit
trust.highlight4Desc: Open-source codebase, transparent security practices. Third-party audits planned post-certification
trust.exploreDesc: View complete security architecture documentation, compliance certificates, data protection measures, and security whitepapers in the Trust Center
trust.reportDesc: Enterprise customers can request a dedicated compliance document package including SOC 2 reports, penetration test summaries, and Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA)

compare.pageDesc: OAuth 2.1 vs SAML 2.0 vs OpenID Connect vs WebAuthn vs LDAP — authentication protocol comparison guide
compare.title: Authentication Protocol Comparison
compare.subtitle: Every authentication protocol has its strengths. This technical comparison helps you understand which protocol fits your use case — from enterprise SSO to passwordless consumer auth.
compare.advantage1Desc: AuthMS integrates OAuth 2.1, OIDC, SAML, WebAuthn, and LDAP in a unified platform — no need to stitch together disparate identity services for different protocol requirements.
compare.advantage2Desc: Different use cases demand different protocols: enterprise workforce (SAML/LDAP), consumer applications (OAuth 2.1 + OIDC), and high-security scenarios (WebAuthn/Passkey). AuthMS lets you mix and match.
compare.advantage3Desc: Built on OAuth 2.1 with mandatory PKCE and FIDO2/WebAuthn for passwordless authentication. Every supported protocol is configured with secure defaults out of the box.
compare.ctaDesc: Our solution architects will provide tailored recommendations based on your business scenario

changelog.pageDesc: AuthMS Changelog — Track feature updates and improvements for every release
changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.subtitle: Track every AuthMS release — see the latest features, improvements, and fixes
changelog.footerText: Want to know more about our plans?

items.0: AuthMS platform officially released with 14 microservices and 544+ API endpoints
items.1: Landing Site launched with dark mode and i18n framework
items.2: Admin Console completed 70% of core features
items.3: Developer Portal provides interactive API documentation and app management

items.0: Added Passkey / WebAuthn passwordless authentication support
items.2: Upgraded API gateway aggregated docs to Scalar UI with dark theme support
items.3: Fixed OAuth callback CORS issues in certain browsers

items.0: DSAR automated processing framework initial release
items.2: Optimized identity service registration performance to 10,000 QPS
items.3: Fixed session service connection pool leak in Redis cluster mode

items.0: Added chunked upload and resumable transfer to storage service
items.1: Enhanced risk scoring model with device fingerprinting and behavioral biometrics

items.0: Added in-app notifications, broadcasts, and targeted messages to notification service
items.1: Communication service template management now supports multi-language variables
items.2: Fixed MFA service TOTP verification failure with large time drift

items.0: Compliance service supports GDPR, SOX, and ISO27001 control mapping
items.1: End-to-end encryption and AES-256 encryption at rest
items.2: gRPC interfaces cover all core services

items.6: Status Page Real Health Data Integration

privacy.pageDesc: AuthMS Privacy Policy — Learn how we collect, use, and protect your personal data
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1Desc: The information we collect includes: personal information you provide when registering (name, email, phone number); log data generated during service usage (IP address, browser type, access time); and security data generated during authentication (login records, device information, MFA configuration)
privacy.section2Desc: We use the collected information to:
privacy.section2Item1: Provide, maintain, and improve our services
privacy.section2Item2: Verify user identity and process authentication requests
privacy.section2Item3: Detect and prevent fraud, abuse, and security incidents
privacy.section2Item5: Comply with legal obligations and respond to regulatory requirements
privacy.section3Desc: We do not sell your personal data. We may share your data in the following circumstances:
privacy.section3Item2: To comply with laws, regulations, or legal process requirements
privacy.section3Item3: To protect the rights, property, or safety of AuthMS, our users, or the public
privacy.section3Item4: With third-party service providers (such as infrastructure providers) under contractual obligations
privacy.section4Desc: We employ industry-leading security measures to protect your data, including: TLS 1.3 encryption in transit, AES-256 encryption at rest, bcrypt/scrypt password hashing, encrypted API key storage, hash-chain audit log verification. We conduct regular security audits and penetration testing to ensure the effectiveness of our security measures
privacy.section5Desc: We retain your personal data until your account is deleted or as required by law. After account deletion, we will delete or anonymize your personal data within 30 days, unless otherwise required by law
privacy.section6Desc: Under applicable data protection laws, you have the following rights: access and export your personal data, correct inaccurate data, delete your data (right to be forgotten), restrict or object to data processing, data portability. You can exercise these rights through your account settings or by contacting
privacy.section7Desc: If you have any questions, comments, or complaints about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at: . Address details are available on the About page

terms.pageDesc: AuthMS Terms of Service — By using our services, you agree to the following terms
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.section1Desc: By using AuthMS services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you are using the services on behalf of an organization, you represent and warrant that you have the authority to bind that organization to these terms
terms.section2Desc: AuthMS provides an enterprise-grade Identity and Access Management (IAM) platform, including but not limited to user authentication, single sign-on, multi-factor authentication, access control, and audit logging. Services are provided as 服务 or private deployment, with specific features depending on your deployment configuration
terms.section3Desc: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account, including:
terms.section3Item1: Providing accurate, complete, and up-to-date account information
terms.section3Item2: Protecting the confidentiality of passwords and security credentials
terms.section3Item3: Promptly updating account information to maintain accuracy
terms.section3Item4: Taking responsibility for all activities that occur under your account
terms.section4Item1: Violate any applicable laws or regulations
terms.section4Item2: Infringe upon the intellectual property or privacy rights of others
terms.section4Item3: Attempt unauthorized access to the service or related systems
terms.section4Item4: Interfere with or disrupt the normal operation of the service or related servers
terms.section4Item5: Abuse APIs or resources beyond reasonable limits
terms.section5Desc: The AuthMS platform, documentation, website, and all related intellectual property are owned by AuthMS. Core modules open-sourced under the Apache 2.0 license are used in accordance with that license. You retain all rights to content you upload or create while using the service
terms.section6Desc: AuthMS offers Free, Pro, and Enterprise subscription plans. See our pricing page for details. Fees are billed per subscription cycle. Online payment and bank transfer are supported. Contact our sales team for custom solutions.
terms.section7Desc: The service is provided "as is" without any express or implied warranties. AuthMS does not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted, timely, secure, or error-free. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, AuthMS shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or punitive damages
terms.section8Desc: Service availability targets: Pro 99.9%, Enterprise 99.99%. These are targets, not contractual guarantees. See Trust Center for full details.
terms.section9Desc: We may update these Terms of Service from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email or in-service notification at least 30 days before taking effect. By continuing to use the service after changes take effect, you accept the modified terms. If you do not agree to the changes, you should stop using the service before the changes take effect

contact.pageDesc: Contact AuthMS — Sales, Technical Demos & Partnerships
contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Whether you need sales inquiries, technical demos, or partnership opportunities, our team is here to help.
contact.enterpriseDesc: Enterprise customers receive dedicated solution consulting, custom deployment planning, and priority technical support.
contact.successDesc: Thank you for your message. Our team will contact you within 1 business day
contact.consentText: By submitting, you agree to our
contact.supportDesc: Please contact us via this form, we will respond shortly.

faq.pageDesc: AuthMS Frequently Asked Questions — Product, Security, Technical Support
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitleIntro: Can't find what you're looking for?
faq.ctaDesc: Our team is ready to answer any questions you may have

questions.a1: AuthMS is an enterprise-grade Identity and Access Management (IAM) platform providing SSO, MFA, multi-tenancy, audit compliance, and developer APIs as a complete identity infrastructure
questions.q2: What authentication methods does AuthMS support?
questions.a2: Supports password authentication, multi-factor authentication (TOTP / SMS / Email / Backup Codes / Push), Passkey / WebAuthn passwordless, OAuth 2.1 / OIDC SSO, SAML 2.0 enterprise SSO, and LDAP / AD directory integration.
questions.q3: What scenarios is AuthMS suitable for?
questions.a3: Enterprise internal application SSO, customer-facing SaaS multi-tenant identity management, API gateway authentication & authorization, and zero-trust security in microservice architecture.
questions.q4: What security certifications does AuthMS have?
questions.a4: AuthMS is pursuing ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certification. Our platform has built-in compliance capabilities covering key requirements of GDPR and China Cybersecurity Classified Protection (Dengbao Level 3). The core codebase is open-source and auditable. See our Security & Compliance page for details.
questions.a5: All data in transit uses TLS 1.3 encryption, and data at rest uses AES-256 encryption. Sensitive credentials (passwords) are hashed with bcrypt/scrypt, and API keys are stored in encrypted form. Audit logs use hash-chain technology to ensure immutability. We follow the principle of least privilege — operations personnel cannot directly access customer data
questions.a6: Yes. Pro and above plans offer private deployment options, including Docker Compose and Kubernetes solutions. Private deployments can run entirely on your own infrastructure without connecting to external networks (after adaptation)
questions.a7: We provide a React SDK. SDKs for Vue, Go, and Java are under development. SDKs cover core features like login/registration, MFA, session management, and permission checks, with complete documentation and example projects
questions.a8: Go-based microservice architecture supporting thousands of concurrent requests per service. High performance and horizontal scaling via PostgreSQL connection pooling, Redis caching, and async message queues.
questions.a9: Yes. You can create and manage Webhooks in the Developer Portal, supporting events like user registration, login, and permission changes. Webhooks support multiple retry strategies and signature verification for delivery reliability
questions.q10: How can I contribute to the community?
questions.a10: The Free plan supports up to 1,000 Monthly Active Users (MAU), without custom branding and domain, no SLA guarantee, and audit logs retained for 7 days. Ideal for individual developers and small projects. See our pricing page for more details.
questions.q11: How does the project ensure security?
questions.a11: OWASP ASVS compliant with WebAuthn / PKCE / hash-chain audit logs, HIBP password strength policy, rate limiting, IP whitelisting. Regular security audits and penetration testing.
questions.a12: Docker Compose one-click deployment (includes PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ). Also supports Kubernetes Helm Charts. Configuration-driven via YAML and environment variables.
questions.a13: 5-tier architecture: Infrastructure (PostgreSQL / Redis / RabbitMQ / MongoDB), Shared (DTOs / Error Codes / Logging), Domain (Business Logic), Middleware (Auth / Rate Limiting / Tracing), Microservices (21 independent services).
questions.a14: Submit technical issues via GitHub Issues, consult project documentation and API Wiki, or participate in community forum discussions.
questions.q15: Where can I check system status?
questions.a15: You can check real-time operational status and historical incident records for all services on the System Status page. We also provide an API endpoint for integration into your own monitoring systems
questions.q16: How do I report a security vulnerability?
questions.a16: If you discover a potential security vulnerability, please email tianv@tianv.com. We commit to acknowledging receipt within 24 hours and providing an initial assessment within 72 hours. See the Security & Compliance page for our detailed vulnerability disclosure process

about.heroDesc: AuthMS was born from a simple belief: identity security should not be a bottleneck for innovation. We are a team of security engineers and product designers building the next generation of identity infrastructure
about.missionDesc: Make enterprise-grade identity security accessible to every application. We believe identity is the cornerstone of the digital world, and security should not come at the cost of user experience
about.value1Desc: Security is not a feature — it's the foundation of AuthMS. From design to deployment, security runs through the entire product lifecycle
about.value2Desc: API-First design philosophy. Every line of code is optimized to reduce developer integration time
about.value3Desc: Open-core architecture, hash-chain audit logs. Customers always maintain control of their data
about.value4Desc: Stay sharp on cutting-edge technology without blindly following trends or becoming complacent
about.milestone1Desc: Core team formed, technical architecture and product roadmap finalized
about.milestone2Desc: Core authentication engine completed, first internal projects onboarded
about.milestone4Desc: 21 microservices, 544+ API endpoints, initiating SOC 2 Type II certification preparation
trust.pageDesc: AuthMS Security & Compliance — Pursuing ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certification
trust.title: Security & Compliance
trust.subtitle: Security is not a feature — it's the foundation of AuthMS. From design to deployment, from code to infrastructure, security permeates every line of code and every decision
trust.highlight1Desc: Data in transit uses TLS 1.3, data at rest uses AES-256 encryption, with additional encryption layers for sensitive fields
trust.highlight2Desc: Every audit log entry is linked to the previous one via a hash chain, ensuring immutability
trust.highlight3Desc: Security in the CI/CD pipeline: automated SAST, dependency scanning, security gates on every commit
trust.highlight4Desc: Open-source codebase, transparent security practices. Third-party audits planned post-certification
trust.exploreDesc: View complete security architecture documentation, compliance certificates, data protection measures, and security whitepapers in the Trust Center
trust.reportDesc: Enterprise customers can request a dedicated compliance document package including SOC 2 reports, penetration test summaries, and Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA)
compare.pageDesc: OAuth 2.1 vs SAML 2.0 vs OpenID Connect vs WebAuthn vs LDAP — authentication protocol comparison guide
compare.title: Authentication Protocol Comparison
compare.subtitle: Every authentication protocol has its strengths. This technical comparison helps you understand which protocol fits your use case — from enterprise SSO to passwordless consumer auth.
compare.advantage1Desc: AuthMS integrates OAuth 2.1, OIDC, SAML, WebAuthn, and LDAP in a unified platform — no need to stitch together disparate identity services for different protocol requirements.
compare.advantage2Desc: Different use cases demand different protocols: enterprise workforce (SAML/LDAP), consumer applications (OAuth 2.1 + OIDC), and high-security scenarios (WebAuthn/Passkey). AuthMS lets you mix and match.
compare.advantage3Desc: Built on OAuth 2.1 with mandatory PKCE and FIDO2/WebAuthn for passwordless authentication. Every supported protocol is configured with secure defaults out of the box.
compare.ctaDesc: Our solution architects will provide tailored recommendations based on your business scenario
changelog.pageDesc: AuthMS Changelog — Track feature updates and improvements for every release
changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.subtitle: Track every AuthMS release — see the latest features, improvements, and fixes
changelog.footerText: Want to know more about our plans?
changelog.releases.v1_0_0.items.0: AuthMS platform officially released with 14 microservices and 544+ API endpoints
changelog.releases.v1_0_0.items.1: Landing Site launched with dark mode and i18n framework
changelog.releases.v1_0_0.items.2: Admin Console completed 70% of core features
changelog.releases.v1_0_0.items.3: Developer Portal provides interactive API documentation and app management
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc5.items.0: Added Passkey / WebAuthn passwordless authentication support
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc5.items.2: Upgraded API gateway aggregated docs to Scalar UI with dark theme support
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc5.items.3: Fixed OAuth callback CORS issues in certain browsers
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc4.items.0: DSAR automated processing framework initial release
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc4.items.2: Optimized identity service registration performance to 10,000 QPS
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc4.items.3: Fixed session service connection pool leak in Redis cluster mode
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc3.items.0: Added chunked upload and resumable transfer to storage service
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc3.items.1: Enhanced risk scoring model with device fingerprinting and behavioral biometrics
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc2.items.0: Added in-app notifications, broadcasts, and targeted messages to notification service
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc2.items.1: Communication service template management now supports multi-language variables
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc2.items.2: Fixed MFA service TOTP verification failure with large time drift
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc1.items.0: Compliance service supports GDPR, SOX, and ISO27001 control mapping
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc1.items.1: End-to-end encryption and AES-256 encryption at rest
changelog.releases.v0_9_0_rc1.items.2: gRPC interfaces cover all core services
roadmap.pageDesc: AuthMS Product Roadmap — See what we're building next
roadmap.title: Product Roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: Our vision is to build the next generation of identity infrastructure. Here's our product roadmap, documenting completed, in-progress, and planned features
roadmap.ctaDesc: We value every user's feedback. If there's a feature you'd like to see, let us know
roadmap.phases.inProgress.items.6: Status Page Real Health Data Integration
roadmap.phases.planned.items.0: AI Copilot Natural Language Policy Queries
privacy.pageDesc: AuthMS Privacy Policy — Learn how we collect, use, and protect your personal data
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1Desc: The information we collect includes: personal information you provide when registering (name, email, phone number); log data generated during service usage (IP address, browser type, access time); and security data generated during authentication (login records, device information, MFA configuration)
privacy.section2Desc: We use the collected information to:
privacy.section2Item1: Provide, maintain, and improve our services
privacy.section2Item2: Verify user identity and process authentication requests
privacy.section2Item3: Detect and prevent fraud, abuse, and security incidents
privacy.section2Item5: Comply with legal obligations and respond to regulatory requirements
privacy.section3Desc: We do not sell your personal data. We may share your data in the following circumstances:
privacy.section3Item2: To comply with laws, regulations, or legal process requirements
privacy.section3Item3: To protect the rights, property, or safety of AuthMS, our users, or the public
privacy.section3Item4: With third-party service providers (such as infrastructure providers) under contractual obligations
privacy.section4Desc: We employ industry-leading security measures to protect your data, including: TLS 1.3 encryption in transit, AES-256 encryption at rest, bcrypt/scrypt password hashing, encrypted API key storage, hash-chain audit log verification. We conduct regular security audits and penetration testing to ensure the effectiveness of our security measures
privacy.section5Desc: We retain your personal data until your account is deleted or as required by law. After account deletion, we will delete or anonymize your personal data within 30 days, unless otherwise required by law
privacy.section6Desc: Under applicable data protection laws, you have the following rights: access and export your personal data, correct inaccurate data, delete your data (right to be forgotten), restrict or object to data processing, data portability. You can exercise these rights through your account settings or by contacting
privacy.section7Desc: If you have any questions, comments, or complaints about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at: . Address details are available on the About page
terms.pageDesc: AuthMS Terms of Service — By using our services, you agree to the following terms
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.section1Desc: By using AuthMS services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you are using the services on behalf of an organization, you represent and warrant that you have the authority to bind that organization to these terms
terms.section2Desc: AuthMS provides an enterprise-grade Identity and Access Management (IAM) platform, including but not limited to user authentication, single sign-on, multi-factor authentication, access control, and audit logging. Services are provided as 服务 or private deployment, with specific features depending on your deployment configuration
terms.section3Desc: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account, including:
terms.section3Item1: Providing accurate, complete, and up-to-date account information
terms.section3Item2: Protecting the confidentiality of passwords and security credentials
terms.section3Item3: Promptly updating account information to maintain accuracy
terms.section3Item4: Taking responsibility for all activities that occur under your account
terms.section4Item1: Violate any applicable laws or regulations
terms.section4Item2: Infringe upon the intellectual property or privacy rights of others
terms.section4Item3: Attempt unauthorized access to the service or related systems
terms.section4Item4: Interfere with or disrupt the normal operation of the service or related servers
terms.section4Item5: Abuse APIs or resources beyond reasonable limits
terms.section5Desc: The AuthMS platform, documentation, website, and all related intellectual property are owned by AuthMS. Core modules open-sourced under the Apache 2.0 license are used in accordance with that license. You retain all rights to content you upload or create while using the service
terms.section6Desc: AuthMS offers Free, Pro, and Enterprise subscription plans. See our pricing page for details. Fees are billed per subscription cycle. Online payment and bank transfer are supported. Contact our sales team for custom solutions.
terms.section7Desc: The service is provided "as is" without any express or implied warranties. AuthMS does not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted, timely, secure, or error-free. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, AuthMS shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or punitive damages
terms.section8Desc: Service availability targets: Pro 99.9%, Enterprise 99.99%. These are targets, not contractual guarantees. See Trust Center for full details.
terms.section9Desc: We may update these Terms of Service from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email or in-service notification at least 30 days before taking effect. By continuing to use the service after changes take effect, you accept the modified terms. If you do not agree to the changes, you should stop using the service before the changes take effect

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Whether you need sales inquiries, technical demos, or partnership opportunities, our team is here to help.
contact.enterpriseDesc: Enterprise customers receive dedicated solution consulting, custom deployment planning, and priority technical support.
contact.successDesc: Thank you for your message. Our team will contact you within 1 business day
contact.consentText: By submitting, you agree to our
contact.supportDesc: Please contact us via this form, we will respond shortly.
faq.pageDesc: AuthMS Frequently Asked Questions — Product, Security, Technical Support
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitleIntro: Can't find what you're looking for?
faq.ctaDesc: Our team is ready to answer any questions you may have
faq.questions.a1: AuthMS is an enterprise-grade Identity and Access Management (IAM) platform providing SSO, MFA, multi-tenancy, audit compliance, and developer APIs as a complete identity infrastructure
faq.questions.q2: What authentication methods does AuthMS support?
faq.questions.a2: Supports password authentication, multi-factor authentication (TOTP / SMS / Email / Backup Codes / Push), Passkey / WebAuthn passwordless, OAuth 2.1 / OIDC SSO, SAML 2.0 enterprise SSO, and LDAP / AD directory integration.
faq.questions.q3: What scenarios is AuthMS suitable for?
faq.questions.a3: Enterprise internal application SSO, customer-facing SaaS multi-tenant identity management, API gateway authentication & authorization, and zero-trust security in microservice architecture.
faq.questions.q4: What security certifications does AuthMS have?
faq.questions.a4: AuthMS is pursuing ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certification. Our platform has built-in compliance capabilities covering key requirements of GDPR and China Cybersecurity Classified Protection (Dengbao Level 3). The core codebase is open-source and auditable. See our Security & Compliance page for details.
faq.questions.a5: All data in transit uses TLS 1.3 encryption, and data at rest uses AES-256 encryption. Sensitive credentials (passwords) are hashed with bcrypt/scrypt, and API keys are stored in encrypted form. Audit logs use hash-chain technology to ensure immutability. We follow the principle of least privilege — operations personnel cannot directly access customer data
faq.questions.a6: Yes. Pro and above plans offer private deployment options, including Docker Compose and Kubernetes solutions. Private deployments can run entirely on your own infrastructure without connecting to external networks (after adaptation)
faq.questions.a7: We provide a React SDK. SDKs for Vue, Go, and Java are under development. SDKs cover core features like login/registration, MFA, session management, and permission checks, with complete documentation and example projects
faq.questions.a8: Go-based microservice architecture supporting thousands of concurrent requests per service. High performance and horizontal scaling via PostgreSQL connection pooling, Redis caching, and async message queues.
faq.questions.a9: Yes. You can create and manage Webhooks in the Developer Portal, supporting events like user registration, login, and permission changes. Webhooks support multiple retry strategies and signature verification for delivery reliability
faq.questions.q10: How can I contribute to the community?
faq.questions.a10: The Free plan supports up to 1,000 Monthly Active Users (MAU), without custom branding and domain, no SLA guarantee, and audit logs retained for 7 days. Ideal for individual developers and small projects. See our pricing page for more details.
faq.questions.q11: How does the project ensure security?
faq.questions.a11: OWASP ASVS compliant with WebAuthn / PKCE / hash-chain audit logs, HIBP password strength policy, rate limiting, IP whitelisting. Regular security audits and penetration testing.
faq.questions.a12: Docker Compose one-click deployment (includes PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ). Also supports Kubernetes Helm Charts. Configuration-driven via YAML and environment variables.
faq.questions.a13: 5-tier architecture: Infrastructure (PostgreSQL / Redis / RabbitMQ / MongoDB), Shared (DTOs / Error Codes / Logging), Domain (Business Logic), Middleware (Auth / Rate Limiting / Tracing), Microservices (21 independent services).
faq.questions.a14: Submit technical issues via GitHub Issues, consult project documentation and API Wiki, or participate in community forum discussions.
faq.questions.q15: Where can I check system status?
faq.questions.a15: You can check real-time operational status and historical incident records for all services on the System Status page. We also provide an API endpoint for integration into your own monitoring systems
faq.questions.q16: How do I report a security vulnerability?
faq.questions.a16: If you discover a potential security vulnerability, please email tianv@tianv.com. We commit to acknowledging receipt within 24 hours and providing an initial assessment within 72 hours. See the Security & Compliance page for our detailed vulnerability disclosure process
